narration: You've probably said it yourself — 'I'll just pay it off next month.' The average American carries $6,360 in credit card debt with that exact plan. Here's what it's really costing them.
visual: Red credit card springs down from top. Below it: italic white quote 'I'll pay it off next month.' A dollar counter animates from $0 up to $6,360 in large red digits on dark background.

narration: At today's average APR of 24%, that balance racks up $1,527 every year in interest. Not on new purchases — just to sit there. That's next-month money, gone forever.
visual: Light background. Dollar signs rain down faintly. A dark card '$6,360 @ 24% APR' scales in. Then a red card '$1,527 / year — JUST TO EXIST' scales in below it.

narration: Here's the psychology — your brain is wired to discount future pain. Scientists call it temporal discounting. Next-month-you feels like a stranger, so future interest feels basically fake.
visual: Dark background. Red brain SVG on the left, an arrow, then a calendar SVG with 'NEXT MONTH' header slides in from the right. Caption fades in: 'Future pain feels unreal.'

narration: Make only the minimum payment and it takes 16 years to clear. By then you've handed your bank $8,400 in pure interest. On money you already spent.
visual: Light background. A red bar chart grows showing cumulative interest paid at Year 0, 4, 8, 12, and 16 — each bar taller than the last. Callout box: '16 YEARS / $8,400 IN PURE INTEREST.'

narration: The average cardholder has three credit cards. Run this math across all three and the interest alone tops $25,000 over a decade. That's not debt — it's a decade-long punishment.
visual: Dark background. Three red credit cards fan out with staggered spring animations. Big text below: '$25K+' in red. Subtitle: 'in interest across just 3 cards.'

narration: The fix is dead simple — set autopay above the minimum today. Even $50 extra a month cuts 16 years down to 4. Your future self is $8,400 richer for one five-minute decision.
visual: Light background. Phone SVG with an autopay screen and green checkmark. A savings counter animates up to $8,400. CTA box: 'Follow for daily money moves.'
